import numpy as np
import os
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge

SEED = 42
N_SAMPLES = 200000
ALPHA = 1.0  # regularization

# Load training data as memory map to avoid full loading
data_dir = os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR', '/data')
train_input_path = os.path.join(data_dir, 'train_input.npy')
train_target_path = os.path.join(data_dir, 'train_target.npy')

print('Memory-mapping training data...')
train_in = np.load(train_input_path, mmap_mode='r')
train_tar = np.load(train_target_path, mmap_mode='r')

N_total = train_in.shape[0]
rng = np.random.RandomState(SEED)
indices = rng.choice(N_total, size=N_SAMPLES, replace=False)
# Sort indices for sequential access efficiency
indices = np.sort(indices)
X_sample = train_in[indices].copy()
y_sample = train_tar[indices].copy()
print(f'Sampled {N_SAMPLES} samples.')

# Fit Ridge regression
model = Ridge(alpha=ALPHA, fit_intercept=True, random_state=SEED)
model.fit(X_sample, y_sample)
W = model.coef_  # shape (128, 124) or (n_outputs, n_features)
b = model.intercept_  # shape (128,)
print(f'Training R^2: {model.score(X_sample, y_sample):.6f}')

# Save linear coefficients
out_dir = 'checkpoints'
os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
np.savez(os.path.join(out_dir, 'linear_model.npz'), W=W, b=b)
print('Linear model saved.')
